programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A medical student is murdered - and Dr Sloan suspects it is only a matter of time before the killer strikes again. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The medic investigates the suspicious deaths of several homeless people, who are seemingly being killed for their organs at a clinic funded by a friend. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble asks newsman Mike Decker for help when the one-armed man he is looking for is spotted in Chicago

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A relaxing fishing trip takes an unexpected turn when Perry and Paul rescue a woman, who is fleeing a murder scene, out of the water

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Alex and Walker assume the role of husband and wife in an exclusive resort and make the most of the lifestyle _ until terrorists move in. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio and the team continue their investigation into the murder of a porn star found strangled in a park. The finger of suspicion points to her agent, who has a distinctly seedy past, and Calleigh finds herself implicated by the discovery of a threatening answering-machine message she left for the victim. However, a forensic blunder puts the case in jeopardy - and Delko gets the blame

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a marine off the coast of Tanzania - a case complicated by the discovery that he had a link to Ziva. The mystery deepens even further with the recovery of wreckage from a sunken freighter. Starring Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble's life is in jeopardy when he learns a secret about a champion Irish setter while working with a champion breeder and trainer

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: While at a restaurant, Perry and Della become caught up in another case after a waitress is run over while fleeing from a stalker, who later turns up dead

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when fellow agent Lara Macy's body is found badly burnt in a campfire on Annapolis beach. According to her laptop she was tailing an unco-operative rape victim, and the prime suspect in the crime is the son of a DC contractor. Gibbs knew Macy years ago, and he finds he can no longer avoid his Mexican past catching up with him as the case progresses. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker helps to protect a family after one of them witnesses a bloody murder. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Dean captures Gibbs in Mexico and hands him over to Paloma Reynosa, head of a drug cartel and daughter of the man he killed. She tries to persuade him to aid her master plan by threatening to kill his father. Alejandro Rivera arrives with a platoon of Mexican troops, but hopes of rescue may be premature. Meanwhile, Ziva is accepted as a US citizen

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse goes missing during a trip into a remote area of the mountains - only to return convinced he has been the victim of an alien abduction. Wil Wheaton and George Takei guest star

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan is drawn into a case of literary murder involving an author who has resorted to desperate measures to cure writer's block. Medical detective drama, with a guest appearance by Shelley Long

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Amanda witnesses a murder on the internet, and soon finds her own life in danger as the killer tracks her down. Detective drama, starring Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members' helicopter crashes while they are out on a mission to rescue a group of lost hikers, and the crash ignites a wildfire, which soon surrounds them. Meanwhile, Sylvester tries to help the injured pilot who is unable to make it to safety. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team protects a key witness who is en route to testify against a deadly drug cartel by taking her on the run - but the criminals are hot on their heels. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects Gibbs's arch-nemesis is behind an explosion at a summit on global terror. Meanwhile, a grief-stricken Fornell struggles to deal with the fatal shooting of his ex-wife. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Joe Spano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A covert identity created for one of Gibbs' undercover cases 20 years previously resurfaces after a murder victim is found to be using the alias. Meanwhile, Bishop becomes anxious at the thought of having to work incognito in the future. Starring Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the case of a marine who dropped dead while scaling a wall on an assault course, and a fellow soldier reveals that he was heavily involved with an underground fight club, where competitors would battle for cash. Meanwhile, having returned to work after compassionate leave, Vance discovers paperwork and a key in his late wife's effects that lead him to a safety deposit box containing a stash of cash

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While transporting the corpse of Lt Gordon Roth to the morgue, a flat tyre brings Ducky and Jimmy to a halt and they are abducted and taken to a remote cabin - where one of their captors wants the post-mortem to take place. Back at the scene, Abby finds a blood sample that does not belong to the victim, who in fact bore little resemblance to the picture of Roth in his old school yearbook. Guest starring Valerie Cruz (The Following)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve accidentally kills a suspect during a routine stake-out, prompting his disillusioned new partner to suggest covering up the death. Medical detective drama, starring Barry Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan and Steve race to find the link between three murder victims before the killer strikes again. Medical detective drama, guest starring George Lazenby

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team flies to Israel, but receives a hostile reception from Ziva's father, who is the head of intelligence agency Mossad. As events unfold, Gibbs learns that Ziva was romantically involved with Rivkin and finds himself forced to make a tough decision. Guest starring Michael Nouri, alongside Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony travels to North Africa on the trail of missing agent Ziva, who is thought to have been tracking down a terrorist leader called Saleem Ulman. Intent on exacting revenge for her apparent death during a flight to Somalia, he is taken hostage by militants and discovers she is still alive - only to be given a potentially catastrophic ultimatum. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his team investigate the bizarre deaths of a marine and two of his friends at a stag party. Elsewhere, Gibbs tries to persuade Vance to allow Ziva back into the NCIS. However, she must first pass a barrage of psychological evaluations. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A blogger who accused the NCIS of a cover-up is found dead, and Gibbs and his team are forced to reopen a closed case to find the killer. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.desc: Nate investigates a knife attack on young girl Aisha, only for the case to take on a personal note when he discovers his new love interest, nurse Maria Joseph, agreed not to report the injury when she treated the wounded girl in hospital. Meanwhile, John begins to sweat when a new body is discovered. Crime drama, starring Sarah Lancashire, James Norton and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The veteran detectives investigate the murder of a palaeontologist at the Natural History Museum, discovering the victim was a respected but outspoken scientist with a knack for rubbing people up the wrong way. When it comes out he was strongly opposed to the museum's sponsorship deal with a large fuel company, the cops soon have plenty of suspects. Amanda Redman stars, with guest appearances by Trevor Bannister, Lucy Brown and Natasha Little

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Concluding part. Jonathan Southwood is taken in for questioning, during which a series of shocking revelations point to the true murderer. Walker faces a race against time to reach Connor, who is working undercover at a private club where Colin Thorpe is putting her professionalism to the ultimate test. Crime drama, starring Victoria Smurfit, David Hayman and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A mummified body found on a derelict aeroplane leads Boyd and the team to a man convicted in a similar case, who Spence gets to know undercover. programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean and Lionel settle down to enjoy a quiet evening, only to find themselves constantly disturbed by ominous noises from the empty house next door. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean begins to think that, 19 months after tying the knot, her marriage may have become stale. She wonders whether a few surprises from Lionel would spice things up, so Alistair, helped by Judith and Sandy, comes up with a plan to give her the treat of a lifetime

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean calls on Alistair's help to do a spot of match-making and Lionel finds himself in the middle of a muddle. Comedy, with Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Death on the Tyne
programme.desc: Comedy mystery following on from Murder on the Blackpool Express, focusing this time on an eventful and bloody overnight ferry trip from Newcastle to Amsterdam. During the turbulent journey, the passengers and crew begin to get bumped off one by one in grizzly circumstances, and everyone seems to have a motive for murder. Johnny Vegas, Sian Gibson, Sheila Reid, Sue Johnston, Georgie Glen, James Fleet, Felicity Montagu and Doon Mackichan are among the all-star cast

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Disaster strikes when Mike's favourite country musician Holly Collins is found electrocuted in what appears to be a drunken accident or possibly suicide. However, Mike finds a note scrawled in lipstick and begins to investigate the dysfunctional band members - Slim Fingers, Waylon Strings and Jesse James. Detective drama, starring Neill Rea

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Patricia Routledge stars as Lancashire housewife Hetty Wainthropp, a part-time Post Office worker with a sideline in sleuthing. In this first episode, the mysterious death of a pensioner leaves a few unanswered questions, but Hetty smells a rat and with her young assistant Geoffrey, sets about solving the mystery the police have little interest in. Drama, also starring Dominic Monaghan and David Benfield

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The moderator-elect of the Church of Scotland and a female companion are found naked in a car with their throats cut, but the church closes ranks as Rebus investigates the background to the case, uncovering long-buried rivalries, lies and grudges within the institution. Meanwhile, a retired police colleague in the early stages of dementia claims he is being poisoned by staff at his nursing home. Drama, starring Ken Stott, with Lorcan Cranitch, Andrew Neil and John Stahl

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Anna Westin, a troubled childhood friend of Wallander's daughter, visits his house late at night begging for his help - but disappears soon afterwards. Meanwhile, an elderly woman's burned body is found in a shallow grave, and when the detective discovers a connection between Anna and the murder victim, he fears she could be the next target of a dangerous religious fanatic. Linda returns to Ystad to help with the investigation, and has life-changing news for her father. Guest starring Lindsay Duncan (Rome), with Kenneth Branagh and Jeany Spark

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team is baffled by the conflicting possible motives after two women are shot dead in a house basement - and the investigation isn't helped by Jack having to work alongside an old flame, the ambitious DI Chrissy Reed. Meanwhile, Leo faces a difficult choice when he is asked to prove his pathologist mentor made a mistake in a case that resulted in a mother being imprisoned for smothering her baby to death. Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves star

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel and Jean return from a weekend away to find Judith's boyfriend has been staying at the house. To add to their woes, Alistair announces he has got engaged to a girl named Mercury - dashing their hopes of his ever becoming their son-in-law. Comedy starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The discovery of two more bodies convinces Heaton his suspect is a serial killer, but the man he sent to prison 20 years before sticks to his story that he is innocent. A retired colleague who worked on the original case reveals that vital evidence was withheld at the time of the trial and the pressure mounts to re-examine the prosecution. Bad Girls' James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. Heaton's first suspect provides an alibi for one of the cold case murders and finds himself in the clear as forensic evidence establishes that all three girls were killed in the same manner. Desperate for his freedom, he cooperates with the police by identifying everyone involved in staging the rave events attended by the victims. When the real killer is named, the race is on to find him before he strikes again. James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a cab driver is badly beaten and his takings stolen, an electronic travel card found near the scene provides a lead, linking the crime to a volunteer worker at a support centre on the Jasmine Allen estate. The suspect is questioned and protests her innocence, but when officers approach her again she runs off, spilling a fair amount of cash in the process

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenager is found unconscious in the street following a party that went disastrously wrong when a known drug dealer turned up causing trouble. Nate takes an instant shine to a paramedic called to attend to the victim, but later finds himself obliged to arrest her brother for his involvement in the incident. Rachel McDowall guest stars

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Charlotte Bronte's novel about an orphan girl who struggles to survive the harsh treatment of a boarding school to embark on a career as a governess. Jane takes up her first position at the home of the alluring and unpredictable Edward Rochester, under the watchful eye of laundrywoman Grace Poole. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Jane begins to wonder about the chemistry between herself and Rochester, who disappoints her when he leaves without warning. However, he returns with a houseful of distinguished guests, including the beautiful Blanche Ingram and her opinionated mother. Things turn sour when a mysterious visitor arrives. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: After fulfilling her dying aunt's last wishes, Jane returns to Thornfield Hall - only to find Mr Rochester is set to wed Blanche. However, after she confesses her love, he shares his true feelings and asks for her hand in marriage instead. While all seems well at first, a ghostly figure begins to torment Jane as the wedding day draws near. Drama, with Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: A clergyman and his two sisters take a heartbroken Jane under their wing after finding her alone and starving on the moors. The governess embarks on a new life as a parish teacher, but thinks she hears Rochester call out her name and feels compelled to return to Thornfield - where the secrets of the North Tower are finally revealed. Starring Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Tide of Life
programme.desc: Gillian Kearney stars in the feature-length period drama following the fortunes of housekeeper Emily Kennedy and her relationships with three very different men. At first she is content working for the McGilby family while her father is away at sea, but finds her life thrown into turmoil by the death of one of her employers. With John Bowler and Ray Stevenson

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara helps a pregnant mother-of-two whose husband has recently died, and is on hand to give her support when a further tragedy drives her and her children from their home. Lucille teaches a health and relationships class at the youth club, only to encounter a furious mother who objects to her daughter attending. Sister Monica Joan goes into hospital for cataract surgery

programme.title: Zen
programme.desc: Detective drama, based on the best-selling novels by Michael Dibdin. Aurelio Zen begins to feel like he is the only honest policeman in Rome, as he sees the city becoming overrun by corruption and deceit - and is plunged into the thick of a political scandal when he is sent to re-investigate a murder in a remote village. As pressure increases from all sides, he finds himself longing for Tania Moretti, the Chief of Police's beautiful assistant - but matters become more complicated when a vengeful gangster starts hounding him. Drama, starring Rufus Sewell and Caterina Murino

programme.title: Death on the Tyne
programme.desc: Comedy mystery following on from Murder on the Blackpool Express, focusing this time on an eventful and bloody overnight ferry trip from Newcastle to Amsterdam. During the turbulent journey, the passengers and crew begin to get bumped off one by one in grizzly circumstances, and everyone seems to have a motive for murder. Johnny Vegas, Sian Gibson, Sheila Reid, Sue Johnston, Georgie Glen, James Fleet, Felicity Montagu and Doon Mackichan are among the all-star cast

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. As the case unfolds, Burke becomes convinced of the writer's guilt, but cannot seem to find sufficient evidence to charge him. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A black man is murdered in a jail cell by a confirmed racist - a crime the prison service blames on overcrowding. However, the judge's probing reveals the institution wasn't operating at full capacity. A drink-driving appeal puts the bench at loggerheads, and Jo distances herself from her former lover. With Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Charlie is appointed to defend an animal rights activist accused of murdering an eminent scientist - a case that will be heard before her father. The defence barrister's support for her client's cause leads her to make a series of unprofessional outbursts in court, while Jo's relationship with Marc Thompson creates tension

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1882, when two boys making their way home from school saw two men in distinctive long black coats shoot Co Kerry farmer and landowner Thomas Browne dead. Despite no murder weapon being recovered, unemployed tenant farmer Sylvester Poff and his cousin James Barrett were arrested, charged and later found guilty of Browne's murder

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 11 of the tournament at the All England Club, featuring Novak Djokovic v Denis Shapovalov and Matteo Berrettini v Hubert Hurkacz in the men's singles semi-finals. Djokovic is the defending champion and favourite to prevail once again, but Shapovalov showed great resilience to defeat Karen Khachanov in five sets in his quarter-final. Seventh seed Berettini is ranked seven places higher than his opponent, but Hurkacz produced a remarkable display in the last eight to defeat Wimbledon great Roger Federer in straight sets

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Jay Blades, Liz Bonnin, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Greg James, Sue Johnston, Guz Khan, Alice Levine, Steph McGovern, Stephen Merchant, Bob Mortimer, Sharon Osbourne, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders, Ranj Singh, Joe Sugg, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack and Sally Bretton return for the domestic comedy, which sees the couple continuing to go from one disaster to the next, while coping with their three kids, Lucy's disapproving parents, Lee's feckless dad Frank and world-weary friends Toby and Anna. In the first episode, Geoffrey reluctantly allows Lee to help Lucy clear up the builder's mess in their new kitchen extension. With Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's plans hit a brick wall when solicitor Ritchie is too busy to take on the custody case, so Ben offers to help. Frankie is nervous when the police arrive with a lead on Nancy's case, Chelsea tries to persuade Gray to let her move in - in her own seductive way - and Bobby is flustered when Dana is arrives at the restaurant. The Walford regulars hear a rumour that football legend Harry Redknapp is making an appearance at the Vic for the Euros. Word is, he's a friend of Rocky - but no one believes a word he says

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Out of Sight (1998)
programme.desc: A charming bank robber breaks out of jail and takes a female US Marshal hostage. Managing to escape, she sets out to track him down as he prepares for one last job - but as the chase intensifies, so do their feelings for each other. Steven Soderbergh's romantic crime drama, based on Elmore Leonard's novel, with George Clooney, Jennifer Lopez, Ving Rhames and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Angela Hartnett is joined by Itamar Srulovich, Sarit Packer, Adam Handling and Vick Hope. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from an antique fair at Newmarket's racecourse, as experts Colin Young and Jonathan Pratt help today's reds and blues

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 12 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the ladies' singles final takes centre stage. Simona Halep took the honours here when the competition was last held two years ago, denying Serena Williams an eighth Wimbledon singles title with an emphatic 6-2, 6-2 victory. However, Halep was unable to defend her title this time around due to a calf injury, while Williams also succumbed to injury after a slip in the first round

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Darkest Hour (2017)
programme.desc: May 1940. As the Nazis advance against Britain's European allies, newly elected prime minister Winston Churchill must decide whether to leave neighbouring countries to their fate, by striking a peace deal with Hitler, or fight on against terrifying odds for the greater good. Double Oscar and Bafta-winning period drama, starring Gary Oldman, Lily James and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lev admits the truth about his sexuality after saving the life of the man he attacked - but a visit from his father changes everything. Tina manages to correctly diagnose a patient from their social media, breaching rules of patient confidentiality, and adopts underhand methods to uncover the truth behind a seemingly happy couple's facade. A patient accuses Charlie of having an affair with his wife. Guest starring Alexei Sayle and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: Weird Science (1985)
programme.desc: Two sexually frustrated teenagers use their computer to design the perfect woman, but are ill prepared for the outcome when their creation comes to life. She decides to make men of them, by putting them in a series of situations that will force them to stand up for themselves. John Hughes' sci-fi comedy, starring Kelly LeBrock, Anthony Michael Hall, Ilan Mitchell-Smith, Robert Downey Jr and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and the Rev Kate Bottley present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, beginning by speaking to people who have found the faith and courage to change their lives, including a young man who has turned his life around after a stint in prison

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley help teams scouring the streets of Hungerford, Berkshire, pick up items to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 13 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles final takes centre stage. All-time greats Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic met once again in the showpiece match when the tournament was last held in 2019, and they served up a classic. The match was eventually decided in the fifth set by the first final set tie-breaker to take place in a Wimbledon final upon the set reaching 12-12, and it was Djokovic who was to eventually take the honours, lifting the trophy for the fifth time in his illustrious career

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Isle of Purbeck in Dorset, looking at the changes the coastline has gone through over the centuries and encountering local wildlife past and present. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the damage that is being caused to the countryside and coast by an influx of visitors and holidaymakers and the latest news on Dwayne Fields' Snowdonia adventure

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the final, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. After a nervy semi-final appearance that saw them overcome a deficit for the first time during the tournament, Gareth Southgate's England players secured their place in the final of a major competition for just the second time in history. Their opponents have a rich pedigree, having won the World Cup four times and the European Championships once, and come into this match on the back of a 32-game unbeaten run. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann

programme.title: The Client (1994)
programme.desc: A Mafia lawyer tells a boy where a politician's body is buried shortly before committing suicide, prompting a team of district attorneys to press the young witness for information. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. Courtroom thriller based on the novel by John Grisham, with Susan Sarandon, Tommy Lee Jones and Brad Renfro

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v England. Another chance to see the showpiece match, as the tournament concluded at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the controversial killing of a police officer which sparked the round-up of dozens of suspects and ended with three men going to the gallows

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp takes a trip along Pontcysyllte aqueduct

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Part two of two. Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This time, the team creates an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. Elsewhere, cameras follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded Second World War bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 11 of the tournament at the All England Club, including the early stages of the opening men's singles semi-final, with the last-four matches in the ladies' doubles and mixed doubles and the quad wheelchair doubles final also set to take place. The top two seeds both fell in the ladies' doubles at this stage two years, with Barbora Krejcikova and Katerina Siniakova losing out to Gabriela Dabrowski and Xu Yifan, and Timea Babos and Kristina Mladenovic suffering defeat at the hands of Hsie Su-wei and Barbora Strycova, In the mixed doubles, Robert Lindstedt and Jelena Ostapenko prevailed against Matwe Middelkoop and Yang Zhaoxuan, and Ivan Dodig and Latisha Chan joined them in the final after a victory over Wesley Koolhof and Kveta Peschke

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of the day's play, where the men's singles semi-finals were scheduled to take centre-stage at the All England Club, with the players hoping to stay in contention to succeed 2019 champion Novak Djokovic. Plus, there is a round-up of the day's headlines, and a preview of tomorrow's ladies' singles final

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don catches up with all his summer jobs in the border and the vegetable garden. Carol Klein gets inspiration for planting combinations at Cothay Manor and Gardens, and Arit Anderson learns about slugs and snails

programme.title: The Moments That Made...
programme.desc: Celebrating the life and career of actor Richard Wilson, featuring an interview with the One Foot in the Grave star and other key people who have been part of his career, including David Tennant and Arabella Weir. He talks about his humble beginnings as a lab technician from Greenock, how he went on to become one of the most recognisable faces in Britain, and why he nearly turned down the role of a lifetime

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Responding to a call of a brutal attack in a residential area of Dudley, West Midlands Police arrive on the scene to find the body of a young man. Soon afterwards, a second man, brought to hospital from the crime scene, is declared dead. Forensic Investigators must now piece together the evidence in this complex murder inquiry

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: The return of the crime drama set in the borderlands between Mexico and America. The Mayans and their Californian town suffer as federal forces crack down on all illegal activity, and grieving Galindo lets his legitimate project dry up too

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: Against better judgement, EZ puts a proposal to Bishop, rebel Adelita is handed to the Mexican authorities, and Coco goes gunning for opioids

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Report on British businesses dealing with post-Brexit trading rules six months after the UK left the EU single market, trying to maintain relationships with their European customers while also seeking new markets further afield. The programme features personal accounts from fishermen in Scotland to entrepreneurs in the home counties

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist looks at attempts to answer one of the most profound questions we can ask - are we alone in the universe? With scientists sending space probes to the furthest reaches of our solar system and beyond, the scientific search for alien life has begun and Brian explains why this quest deserves to be taken seriously, and explores the chances of extraterrestrials being found

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Odd animal transportation tales, including the poisonous frog helping to make air travel more safe and a convoy of caterpillars on the lookout for a new home

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Archie asks for a farm den where he can grow vegetables and look after his chickens

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Two teams fight it out to dodge detention, and put the cool back into school

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Lindsey begins her final challenge as she prapares to showcase her skills on the racetrack

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Young cook Omari makes mega-loaded fries, crunchy potato wedges topped with spicy flavours that are the perfect snack to give the family all the energy they need to outwit one another during their game of mega hide-and-seek

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a quest to find the deadliest prehistoric aquatic monster

programme.title: How to Build a Dinosaur
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts discovers how to put a dinosaur skeleton back together, complete with muscles, correct posture and, in some cases, the correct skin colour. The creatures died out 65 million years ago and full skeletons are rarely found

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: The final episode focuses on life on the longest mountain range in the world, the Andes, home to astonishing hidden worlds, and extraordinary animals and people. Cameras follow a female puma and her three cubs as they hunt in the mountains of the frozen south, and the descendants of the Inca gather in an ancient ceremony to build a bridge made from woven grass

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 12 of the Grand Slam tournament at the All England Club, featuring the opening wheelchair finals

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Days (1956)
programme.desc: Victorian gentleman Phileas Fogg wagers he and his loyal valet Passepartout can circumnavigate the globe in only 80 days. Oscar-winning period adventure, based on Jules Verne's novel, starring David Niven, Cantinflas and Shirley MacLaine, with cameo appearances by stars including Noel Coward, Marlene Dietrich, Frank Sinatra, Buster Keaton and John Gielgud

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: The series turns to animals that fly, including footage of half a million mother bats, an agile sparrowhawk raiding for food, and large beetles taking to the sky as part of their mating ritual. The programme also reveals how peregrine falcons are able to reach speeds of 200mph, and why an albatross's nose helps it to fly. Narrated by Suranne Jones

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: The BBC's favourite wildlife presenters look back through the archives as they share their most memorable adventures in Britain and Ireland. Chris Packham remembers a recent filming trip where he had the privilege to spot some extremely rare birds circling the skies above his home county of Hampshire - white-tailed eagles. Gordon Buchanan recalls joining a fishing trawler to film killer whales off the coast of Shetland, while Vianet Djenguet describes his first sight of a cuckoo in Norfolk and Steve Backshall recalls filming seahorses

programme.title: Hampton Court Palace Garden Festival
programme.desc: Coverage of the annual flower show

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further live coverage on day 12 of the Grand Slam tournament at the All England Club, where doubles finals are set to take place. Juan Sebastian Cabal and Robert Farah took the men's title last on this day two years ago after a hard-fought 6-7, 7-6, 7-6, 6-7, 6-3 victory over Nicolas Mahut and Edouard Roger-Vasselin

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents highlights of the penultimate day's play, where the ladies' singles final was the main attraction on Centre Court. The host also casts her eye over the doubles finals, and presents a round-up of the latest news, as the famous event draws ever closer to a finish

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sarah Millican, Cariad Lloyd and Alice Levine join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Objects and Ornaments'. During the course of the show, the panellists discover where to find an actual UFO and meet the world's best-dressed crab. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: Pavarotti in Hyde Park
programme.desc: Italian tenor Luciano Pavarotti's 1991 free concert to celebrate 30 years in opera, when he performed arias by Verdi, Puccini, Bizet and Wagner for a crowd estimated at 125,000, who turned out despite an incessant downpour. He was accompanied by the 82-member Philharmonia Orchestra and the 120-voice Philharmonia Chorus

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Highlights of the second one-day international in the three-match series, which took place at Lord's

programme.title: Golf: The Scottish Open
programme.desc: Action from day three of this year's tournament, which took place at The Renaissance Club in North Berwick

programme.title: Trumbo (2015)
programme.desc: Biopic of the acclaimed Hollywood screenwriter Dalton Trumbo, who was jailed and blacklisted in the 1940s for his left-wing political views. On his release from prison, he manages to continue writing using a series of pseudonyms, and his newfound success leads many to question the validity of the blacklist. Biopic of the writer, starring Bryan Cranston, Diane Lane and Helen Mirren

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the Clovelly Bloomers, who are determined to win gold despite having to garden in the extremely steep landscape of their ancient Devon fishing village

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don catches up with all his summer jobs in the border and the vegetable garden. Carol Klein gets inspiration for planting combinations at Cothay Manor and Gardens, and Arit Anderson learns about slugs and snails

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison celebrate the 150th anniversary of Wimbledon Common, encountering the horseback patrols that enforce the Common's 57 bylaws and discovering how the Wombles have inspired a whole new generation of ecologists. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to protect the world's oceans are delivering for UK waters, and Dwayne Fields leads four youngsters at a crossroad in their lives on an expedition into the Welsh wilderness

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway explores the dramatic landscape of the north Gower saltmarshes, where he learns of the magical and mystical fairy folk, helps a sheep farmer round up his flock and even has a go at weaving. Plus, marine biologist Lizzie Daly tracks trout and Matt Bassett rides the waves on a new 'adaptive' surfboard at Caswell Bay

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray take a look at the now blooming lovely wildflower bank that Chris Beardshaw cleared and weeded and sowed in 2019. Kirsty Wilson is in her pretty and productive allotment, repurposing an old wine box to make into a trendy planter for some of her purple produce

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 13 of the Grand Slam tournament at the All England Club, featuring the remaining wheelchair finals

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Cheddar Gorge in Somerset to hear the origins of the hymn Rock of Ages, while Gemma Hunt meets worship leader Martin Smith and new Christian group Bright City

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose heads for Swaledale, joining farmers who look after rare upland hay meadows and enters the cake-making contest at Muker Show using a pressure cooker he has taken on a climb of Mount Everest. Paul also goes underground to try and find rare industrial artefacts left behind by lead miners in the 19th century and meets All Creatures Great and Small star Peter Davison

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts search for items at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales, where David Harper looks at vintage Second World War posters and Claire Rawle is surprised by a valuable watch

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Highlands, new entrant crofters Lynn and Sandra welcome more new sheep and take delivery of a batch of hens that they hope will lay the foundations of an 'egg empire'. In Northern Ireland, the Rodgers host a barbecue for the local Young Farmers Club, but it is no ordinary flame-grilled affair, while Raymond and Johnny gather the flock to wean this year's lambs in the Cairngorms

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further live coverage on day 13 of the Grand Slam tournament at the All England Club, where the concluding doubles finals are set to take place. Hsieh Su-wei and Barbora Strycova concluded the tournament's Centre Court proceedings two years ago with a 6-2, 6-4 ladies' doubles triumph over Gabriela Dabrowski and Xu Yifan. Over on the No.1 Court, Ivan Dodig and Latisha Chan prevailed 6-2, 6-3 over Robert Lindstedt and Jelena Ostapenko in the mixed doubles

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters and crew reflect on memorable visits to India, Bhutan and Bangladesh, with Liz Bonnin remembering a remarkable morning migration by thousands of cranes to the small village of Khichan in Rajasthan. Chris Packham looking back on a tiger-spotting trip, while Steve Backshall and Gordon Buchanan recall an expedition looking for tigers in the mountains of Bhutan

programme.title: The Duchess (2008)
programme.desc: Fact-based period drama, chronicling the life of Georgiana Spencer who became Duchess of Devonshire in 1774 at the age of 17. Adored by the public for her extravagant lifestyle, Spencer endured a troubled marriage and was eventually driven into the arms of Charles Grey, a senior Whig politician. Starring Keira Knightley, Ralph Fiennes, Dominic Cooper and Charlotte Rampling

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding and guests look back at highlights of the men's singles final, where the third Grand Slam title of the year was set to be decided. Plus, news from the rest of the day's finals as the famous tournament was brought to a conclusion, and a review of this year's staging of the event

programme.title: Golf: The Scottish Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from day four of this year's tournament, which took place at The Renaissance Club in North Berwick. England's Aaron Rai was the victorious player here in 2020

programme.title: Every Day (2018)
programme.desc: Sixteen-year-old Rhiannon falls in love with a mysterious soul named A, who inhabits a different body every day. The two work to find one another daily, not knowing what or who the next 24 hours will bring. Romantic fantasy drama, starring Angourie Rice and Justice Smith

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con
programme.desc: The rise and fall of internet wellness guru Belle Gibson, who cultivated an adoring online following with the story that she had successfully treated her own cancer by eating a plant-based diet and using alternative natural therapies. There was one major problem with Belle's story - she never had cancer

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Given the hospital's chequered history, everyone is anxious and on high alert as the Care Quality Commission conducts an emergency inspection. Josh's mother puts pressure on his age-gap romance with Ange, while doubt is cast on a patient Rich Peterson's claim to have been bitten by a dog

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: The Herculis EBS. Coverage of the seventh meeting of the campaign, held at Stade Louis II, as the athletes fine-tune preparations for the Olympics. Uganda's Joshua Cheptegei broke the world 5,000m record here last year, and world-lead times were set in a number of other events, including the men's 200m and the women's 5,000m

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner presents the edition first aired on April 11 1991, featuring performances by the Wonderstuff, Alison Limerick, Gary Clail On-U Sound System, Mike & the Mechanics, Dannii Minogue, Madonna, Monie Love versus Adeva, Chesney Hawkes and the Shamen

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles presents the edition first aired on April 18 1991, featuring performances by James, Orchestral Manoeuvres in the Dark, Black Box, the Mock Turtles, Zucchero & Paul Young, Cher, the Clash, Chesney Hawkes and the Simpsons

programme.title: The Beatles: Made on Merseyside
programme.desc: Few dispute that the Beatles defined 1960s music and popular culture like no other band, but how John, Paul, George and Ringo made the journey from Merseyside teenagers to international pop stars is less known. Director Alan Byron's documentary recounts the rise of the band as American rock 'n' roll and rhythm and blues dragged post-war Liverpool into one of the most vibrant music cities ever with the Mersey Beat sound

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyle's comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: Sings the Beatles
programme.desc: A look back through the BBC's archives at memorable covers of classic Beatles songs. Included are a performance of Day Tripper by Sandie Shaw, Shirley Bassey's version of Something, the Carpenters' rendition of Help!, Oasis's reinvention of I Am the Walrus, and Joe Cocker's chart-topping interpretation of With a Little Help from My Friends. Plus, Paul McCartney's solo performance of Lady Madonna

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner presents the edition first aired on April 11 1991, featuring performances by the Wonderstuff, Alison Limerick, Gary Clail On-U Sound System, Mike & the Mechanics, Dannii Minogue, Madonna, Monie Love versus Adeva, Chesney Hawkes and the Shamen

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles presents the edition first aired on April 18 1991, featuring performances by James, Orchestral Manoeuvres in the Dark, Black Box, the Mock Turtles, Zucchero & Paul Young, Cher, the Clash, Chesney Hawkes and the Simpsons

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke explores the riches and unique legacy of Greek art, telling the story of its mysterious origins as he starts his journey at the palace of Knossos in Crete. He then travels to Santorini and finds gold in the fabled stronghold of Mycenae, and uncovers the beginnings of a defining spirit in Greek art - embracing mythology, a passion for symmetry, and an obsession with the human body

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop present an exhaustive guide to East Anglia, from the Wash to Canvey Island. Neil takes to the seas to record new stories, and revisits some of the best Coast features from the past 10 years as he visits England's biggest grey seal colony, and witnesses extreme erosion at Happisburgh. He also takes a trip on board a floating relic of the herring-fishing industry, and finishes his exploration at Orford Ness, where one of the region's most distinctive landmarks is at risk of becoming a victim of the constant battle between land and sea

programme.title: Beck
programme.desc: Oskar and Josef make a macabre discovery in the boot of a car. But things are not what they seem and it takes an emotional toll on the Beck group. In Swedish

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter continues her forensic look at the Ruth Ellis case by focusing on the trial, which took just a day and a half, and saw her lawyer pursue a risky defence strategy

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: Following the murder of public prosecutor Peter Schultz, it becomes clear that Emilie Zeuthen's kidnapper will stop at nothing to recover his debt. Meanwhile, the prime minister's team tries to limit his public involvement in the affair of the kidnapping, but it proves difficult when new information emerges. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: Another failed attempt to capture their man leaves Borch and Lund with a dilemma. Meanwhile, further links to an unresolved police case continue to emerge, shedding new light on the investigation while marring the prime minister's political life. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol and Nikolaj Lie Kaas. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: The kidnapper continues to insist that he is seeking to recover his debt, and following a request for Robert Zeuthen's life in exchange for Emilie's, police set out to fulfil the instructions, while Prime Minister Kamper makes a dramatic decision. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: Police are led to the Jutland region by their investigation. Here, Borch and Lund make an eerie discovery and find themselves in danger. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Kamper hesitates in implementing his decision following doubts about Emilie's fate, and it comes to light that Ussing has been harbouring a secret. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: Sacred Music: The Story of Allegri's Miserere
programme.desc: Simon Russell Beale explores the 300-year history of the sacred choral work, which was decreed by the Pope to be too beautiful to be heard outside the Sistine Chapel. An English organist, a choirmaster from Worcester and the great composers Mozart and Mendelssohn all played a part in the transformation of Allegri's original 17th-century composition into the haunting music known today. Featuring a performance of the piece by award-winning choir the Sixteen, conducted by Harry Christophers

programme.title: Vienna Summer Night Concert 2021
programme.desc: Summer-night concert from the grounds of the Schoenbrunn Palace in Vienna

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Laurence Rees examines the clash of values between Josef Stalin and Winston Churchill and how they were played out over the future of Poland in 1944. Records of secret meetings reveal the British prime minister thought he could persuade the Soviets to allow an independent government in the country the UK had gone into the war to protect, but the efforts to play politics threatened the integrity of the alliance

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Chris Lintott discover how the search for extra-terrestrial life in the universe has been reported by the BBC over the past six decades, from the birth of radio astronomy in the 1960s to the discovery of the first planets outside of our own solar system in the 1990s, and right up to probes exploring our neighbouring planets in the present day

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The scientists scouring the universe for signs of extraterrestrial life, including the film researchers at the Green Bank Telescope searching for radio signals from 'Tabby's Star', a star so mysterious some scientists believe it might be surrounded by a Dyson Sphere - a vast energy collector built by advanced aliens. The film also features the 2006 discovery by husband and wife team Duncan Lorimer and Maura McLaughlin of an enigmatic signal from space, known as a fast radio burst, and its subsequent explanations

programme.title: The Chauffeur's Tale
programme.desc: With millions of fans worldwide, and a career spanning more than seven decades, Barbara Cartland was not only a record-breaking novelist, but also a colourful public figure in her own right. Robert Alderson, former chauffeur and butler to the writer, discusses how she maintained the sheen on her own glossy character

programme.title: Missions

programme.title: Sacred Music: The Story of Allegri's Miserere
programme.desc: Simon Russell Beale explores the 300-year history of the sacred choral work, which was decreed by the Pope to be too beautiful to be heard outside the Sistine Chapel. An English organist, a choirmaster from Worcester and the great composers Mozart and Mendelssohn all played a part in the transformation of Allegri's original 17th-century composition into the haunting music known today. Featuring a performance of the piece by award-winning choir the Sixteen, conducted by Harry Christophers

programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of trees to see how we can help look after them and our planet. We’ll discover a tree’s leaves make energy using sunlight, so they can keep growing. We learn that a tree is alive, it drinks and breathes and grows just like we do! Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Betty the Mouse’s little ones see a big shadow from their cosy nest in the potting shed, and all agree it must be a dinosaur. Word spreads around the garden and reaches Tilly the Tortoise, who is on a visit for the holidays. She can’t believe her luck – she loves dinosaurs! Tilly heads off on a search around the garden, bumping into Casey the Badger and Rosie the Rabbit, but no one has seen it. Auntie the Tawny Owl points her in the right direction – back to the potting shed! Will she ever find the dinosaur?

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 12-month-old Tomos and Sabrina as they discover the world around them and get into a routine. Tomos and Sabrina both start their day with a scrummy breakfast and then get ready to go out! Tomos goes for a walk with his family and has a small nap in his pushchair. Sabrina plays on the slide in the garden with mum and dad. After working up an appetite, they head inside for lunch. Meanwhile in the garden, Tomos and his sisters cook up a storm in their mud kitchen. After a lovely day with their families, Tomos and Sabrina both get ready for bed. Tomos has a bath and Sabrina has a soothing massage and a story before bed. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome little ones Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, River, Araiya and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag are numbered building blocks, and all the parents and little ones have different objects and toys that are themed around numbers. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate number-themed objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The traditional children’s nursery rhyme One, Two, Buckle My Shoe features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring numbers too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.
