programme.desc: Lawrie the Little Owl likes the quiet life. He has no time for adventures and no longing to explore anywhere outside of the garden. But Lawrie’s world gets turned upside down when Parker the Pheasant mistakenly overhears Lawrie reciting a poem and assumes he’s a famous explorer. Rumour spreads around the garden, and it’s not long before everyone is rushing to get a glimpse of this most famous of owls. It’s not the perfect quiet morning that poor old Lawrie had in mind.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 13-month-old Gia and twins, Chester and Elias, as they discover the world around them and find out what words they are learning. Both families are playing at home, talking about colours and a four-legged friend. They all head to baby classes, where they use music to practice their words, and some puppets come along to help them learn! Back at home, Chester and Elias play in the garden with their brother, going up and down the slide. Gia takes mum and granddad for a walk in the woods, where they see lots of beautiful things - they even find a bird! Finally, Chester and Elias play a counting game and Gia makes a wonderful word wall in the garden using pictures of things they saw that day. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Sullivan, Araiya, River, Taiba, Luna and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel has got a shopping basket inside his bag. All the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around a trip to the shops too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate shopping-themed toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to discover with their baby! Some viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring shopping too. The fun children’s song Shopping Day features, as does a story about Baby Bear at the shops. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley and Aimee head to a land of knights and castles, and Pink Bird, dresses as the lonely Lady of Shalott and sits and weaves in her island castle

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari is fascinated by how a pinwheel moves in the wind - can she make one of her own?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and their cousins make Dad and Uncle Stripe play a game of Horsey Ride.

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour green, from grass to grapes. We explore the meaning of being green, and the episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour green.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a robot workshop where they design farming robots that help farmers grow crops. She meets three farming robots, Tom, Wilma and Richard that work together on the farm to help find the weeds in the fields. Maddie learns that Tom, a scanning robot, can see things we cannot see, using special cameras to scan and take photos of the weeds, animals, insects and crops in the field. Maddie then discovers how Tom sends pictures and information to an artificial intelligence computer called Wilma to build a map of the field. Finally, Maddie meets another Richard, a robot who uses the map to find and destroy the weeds. Maddie then visits a printing workshop to see how a 3D printer makes things. At the workshop, they design glasses frames to wear and Maddie uses her special camera to see how the 3D printing works by building up layers stacked on top of each other.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran play ‘holidays' in the park and have a stay-at-home adventure.

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Part two of two. Grannie Island is unhappy when Granddad Island arrives with Granma Mainland for the wedding, but the little girl succeeds in making her enjoy the celebrations

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The librarian loses his big reference book and Sarah thinks her toy periscope might help with the search

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The duo go to the library, but Lola cannot find her favourite book

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: When Mo wakes up with a cold, who s there to take over mum duties? Tee of course!

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka's trumpet makes a funny noise, and the Pontipines count to three. Derek Jacobi narrates

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Stephen Graham reads 'The Snuggle Sandwich', by Malachy Doyle and Gwen Millward

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-time investor takes on a garden level flat in London's Crystal Palace, Dion Dublin meets a lorry driver who's renovating a mid-terrace house in Stoke-on Trent, and in Strood, Kent, a Navy reservist transforms a three-storey townhouse into a stylish holiday let

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1882, when two boys making their way home from school saw two men in distinctive long black coats shoot Co Kerry farmer and landowner Thomas Browne dead. Despite no murder weapon being recovered, unemployed tenant farmer Sylvester Poff and his cousin James Barrett were arrested, charged and later found guilty of Browne's murder

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A farmer and his daughter are both rushed to A&E with injuries just as their family is busy with sheep-shearing season, and a crofter has to have a kidney removed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes a look at the rarest and most unique items bought by past contestants and also finds out about a rare book on birds worth millions of pounds. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp talks to Charles Hanson about Faberge flowers, and Charlie Ross explores a unique collection of self-playing instruments

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 11 of the tournament at the All England Club, featuring Novak Djokovic v Denis Shapovalov and Matteo Berrettini v Hubert Hurkacz in the men's singles semi-finals. Djokovic is the defending champion and favourite to prevail once again, but Shapovalov showed great resilience to defeat Karen Khachanov in five sets in his quarter-final. Seventh seed Berettini is ranked seven places higher than his opponent, but Hurkacz produced a remarkable display in the last eight to defeat Wimbledon great Roger Federer in straight sets

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Jay Blades, Liz Bonnin, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Greg James, Sue Johnston, Guz Khan, Alice Levine, Steph McGovern, Stephen Merchant, Bob Mortimer, Sharon Osbourne, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders, Ranj Singh, Joe Sugg, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack and Sally Bretton return for the domestic comedy, which sees the couple continuing to go from one disaster to the next, while coping with their three kids, Lucy's disapproving parents, Lee's feckless dad Frank and world-weary friends Toby and Anna. In the first episode, Geoffrey reluctantly allows Lee to help Lucy clear up the builder's mess in their new kitchen extension. With Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's plans hit a brick wall when solicitor Ritchie is too busy to take on the custody case, so Ben offers to help. Frankie is nervous when the police arrive with a lead on Nancy's case, Chelsea tries to persuade Gray to let her move in - in her own seductive way - and Bobby is flustered when Dana is arrives at the restaurant. The Walford regulars hear a rumour that football legend Harry Redknapp is making an appearance at the Vic for the Euros. Word is, he's a friend of Rocky - but no one believes a word he says

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.title: Out of Sight (1998)
programme.desc: A charming bank robber breaks out of jail and takes a female US Marshal hostage. Managing to escape, she sets out to track him down as he prepares for one last job - but as the chase intensifies, so do their feelings for each other. Steven Soderbergh's romantic crime drama, based on Elmore Leonard's novel, with George Clooney, Jennifer Lopez, Ving Rhames and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Angela Hartnett is joined by Itamar Srulovich, Sarit Packer, Adam Handling and Vick Hope. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of economic recipes, including a Bake Off masterclass featuring Sussex suet pond pudding, and a James Martin tomato soup with garlic bread

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Pizza chefs Jez and Ieuz continue their adventure across Europe, where they cook pizzas for locals in Bardi, Italy, before meeting one of their heroes in Rome

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 12 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the ladies' singles final takes centre stage. Simona Halep took the honours here when the competition was last held two years ago, denying Serena Williams an eighth Wimbledon singles title with an emphatic 6-2, 6-2 victory. However, Halep was unable to defend her title this time around due to a calf injury, while Williams also succumbed to injury after a slip in the first round

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Darkest Hour (2017)
programme.desc: May 1940. As the Nazis advance against Britain's European allies, newly elected prime minister Winston Churchill must decide whether to leave neighbouring countries to their fate, by striking a peace deal with Hitler, or fight on against terrifying odds for the greater good. Double Oscar and Bafta-winning period drama, starring Gary Oldman, Lily James and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lev admits the truth about his sexuality after saving the life of the man he attacked - but a visit from his father changes everything. Tina manages to correctly diagnose a patient from their social media, breaching rules of patient confidentiality, and adopts underhand methods to uncover the truth behind a seemingly happy couple's facade. A patient accuses Charlie of having an affair with his wife. Guest starring Alexei Sayle and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: Weird Science (1985)
programme.desc: Two sexually frustrated teenagers use their computer to design the perfect woman, but are ill prepared for the outcome when their creation comes to life. She decides to make men of them, by putting them in a series of situations that will force them to stand up for themselves. John Hughes' sci-fi comedy, starring Kelly LeBrock, Anthony Michael Hall, Ilan Mitchell-Smith, Robert Downey Jr and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and the Rev Kate Bottley present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley help teams scouring the streets of Hungerford, Berkshire, pick up items to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Iolo's Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Pembrokeshire's stunning landscape and goes in search of its hidden wildlife spots, finding a rare butterfly and an old oil storage site where hundreds of orchids have bloomed. Iolo also walks round St David's Head, where he finds fearsome looking beetles and Britain's only venomous snake

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 13 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles final takes centre stage. All-time greats Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic met once again in the showpiece match when the tournament was last held in 2019, and they served up a classic. The match was eventually decided in the fifth set by the first final set tie-breaker to take place in a Wimbledon final upon the set reaching 12-12, and it was Djokovic who was to eventually take the honours, lifting the trophy for the fifth time in his illustrious career

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Isle of Purbeck in Dorset, looking at the changes the coastline has gone through over the centuries and encountering local wildlife past and present. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the damage that is being caused to the countryside and coast by an influx of visitors and holidaymakers and the latest news on Dwayne Fields' Snowdonia adventure

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the final, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. After a nervy semi-final appearance that saw them overcome a deficit for the first time during the tournament, Gareth Southgate's England players secured their place in the final of a major competition for just the second time in history. Their opponents have a rich pedigree, having won the World Cup four times and the European Championships once, and come into this match on the back of a 32-game unbeaten run. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann

programme.title: The Client (1994)
programme.desc: A Mafia lawyer tells a boy where a politician's body is buried shortly before committing suicide, prompting a team of district attorneys to press the young witness for information. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. Courtroom thriller based on the novel by John Grisham, with Susan Sarandon, Tommy Lee Jones and Brad Renfro

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v England. Another chance to see the showpiece match, as the tournament concluded at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Highlights of recent events

programme.title: A View from the Euros
programme.desc: The team highlight their favourite moments of Euro 2020, looking back on everything they've enjoyed from the past six weeks before falling head over heels for their unexpected heroes in Holiday Romance. Robert Borthwick goes sifting through the social media EuroTrash and the group decide what moment should be preserved forever in the Time Capsule. They end by picking their favourites for One Night Only as the tournament reaches the final

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: Amy Irons and David Farrell chat to singer Jack Savoretti about his new album Europiana, Jodie McCallum talks to singer Tom Odell about filming his new music video in an empty Wembley Stadium, and we chat all the latest Love Island theories with former contestant Amy Hart

Seventh seed Berettini is ranked seven places higher than his opponent, but Hurkacz produced a remarkable display in the last eight to defeat Wimbledon great Roger Federer in straight sets

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Jay Blades, Liz Bonnin, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Greg James, Sue Johnston, Guz Khan, Alice Levine, Steph McGovern, Stephen Merchant, Bob Mortimer, Sharon Osbourne, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders, Ranj Singh, Joe Sugg, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack and Sally Bretton return for the domestic comedy, which sees the couple continuing to go from one disaster to the next, while coping with their three kids, Lucy's disapproving parents, Lee's feckless dad Frank and world-weary friends Toby and Anna. In the first episode, Geoffrey reluctantly allows Lee to help Lucy clear up the builder's mess in their new kitchen extension. With Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's plans hit a brick wall when solicitor Ritchie is too busy to take on the custody case, so Ben offers to help. Frankie is nervous when the police arrive with a lead on Nancy's case, Chelsea tries to persuade Gray to let her move in - in her own seductive way - and Bobby is flustered when Dana is arrives at the restaurant. The Walford regulars hear a rumour that football legend Harry Redknapp is making an appearance at the Vic for the Euros. Word is, he's a friend of Rocky - but no one believes a word he says

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Out of Sight (1998)
programme.desc: A charming bank robber breaks out of jail and takes a female US Marshal hostage. Managing to escape, she sets out to track him down as he prepares for one last job - but as the chase intensifies, so do their feelings for each other. Steven Soderbergh's romantic crime drama, based on Elmore Leonard's novel, with George Clooney, Jennifer Lopez, Ving Rhames and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Angela Hartnett is joined by Itamar Srulovich, Sarit Packer, Adam Handling and Vick Hope. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from an antique fair at Newmarket's racecourse, as experts Colin Young and Jonathan Pratt help today's reds and blues

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 12 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the ladies' singles final takes centre stage. Simona Halep took the honours here when the competition was last held two years ago, denying Serena Williams an eighth Wimbledon singles title with an emphatic 6-2, 6-2 victory. However, Halep was unable to defend her title this time around due to a calf injury, while Williams also succumbed to injury after a slip in the first round

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Darkest Hour (2017)
programme.desc: May 1940. As the Nazis advance against Britain's European allies, newly elected prime minister Winston Churchill must decide whether to leave neighbouring countries to their fate, by striking a peace deal with Hitler, or fight on against terrifying odds for the greater good. Double Oscar and Bafta-winning period drama, starring Gary Oldman, Lily James and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lev admits the truth about his sexuality after saving the life of the man he attacked - but a visit from his father changes everything. Tina manages to correctly diagnose a patient from their social media, breaching rules of patient confidentiality, and adopts underhand methods to uncover the truth behind a seemingly happy couple's facade. A patient accuses Charlie of having an affair with his wife. Guest starring Alexei Sayle and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: Weird Science (1985)
programme.desc: Two sexually frustrated teenagers use their computer to design the perfect woman, but are ill prepared for the outcome when their creation comes to life. She decides to make men of them, by putting them in a series of situations that will force them to stand up for themselves. John Hughes' sci-fi comedy, starring Kelly LeBrock, Anthony Michael Hall, Ilan Mitchell-Smith, Robert Downey Jr and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and the Rev Kate Bottley present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, beginning by speaking to people who have found the faith and courage to change their lives, including a young man who has turned his life around after a stint in prison

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley help teams scouring the streets of Hungerford, Berkshire, pick up items to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 13 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles final takes centre stage. All-time greats Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic met once again in the showpiece match when the tournament was last held in 2019, and they served up a classic. The match was eventually decided in the fifth set by the first final set tie-breaker to take place in a Wimbledon final upon the set reaching 12-12, and it was Djokovic who was to eventually take the honours, lifting the trophy for the fifth time in his illustrious career

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Isle of Purbeck in Dorset, looking at the changes the coastline has gone through over the centuries and encountering local wildlife past and present. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the damage that is being caused to the countryside and coast by an influx of visitors and holidaymakers and the latest news on Dwayne Fields' Snowdonia adventure

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the final, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. After a nervy semi-final appearance that saw them overcome a deficit for the first time during the tournament, Gareth Southgate's England players secured their place in the final of a major competition for just the second time in history. Their opponents have a rich pedigree, having won the World Cup four times and the European Championships once, and come into this match on the back of a 32-game unbeaten run. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann

programme.title: The Client (1994)
programme.desc: A Mafia lawyer tells a boy where a politician's body is buried shortly before committing suicide, prompting a team of district attorneys to press the young witness for information. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. Courtroom thriller based on the novel by John Grisham, with Susan Sarandon, Tommy Lee Jones and Brad Renfro

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v England. Another chance to see the showpiece match, as the tournament concluded at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Thursday's business in the House of Lords, including topical questions to Government ministers, statements on Covid-19 restrictions in England and in schools, a debate on trading relationships with Commonwealth countries and a debate on improving women's health outcomes.

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: A storm is brewing in the South China Sea. China claims large swathes of it as its own, but President Biden has recently warned that it must be open for trade and navigation. Caught in the middle are Filipino fishermen, who increasingly find themselves shadowed by the Chinese coastguard and militia boats as they try to earn a living. Howard Johnson sails to Scarborough Shoal, a large coral reef 120 nautical miles off the coast of the Philippines, to discover how far China is prepared to go to assert its power and influence in the region.

programme.desc: In our culture of 24/7 news and trending social media reactions, it sometimes takes a novelist's eye to chart the deeper, current events swirling beneath society's surface. Lionel Shriver, is a British-based American writer whose fiction has addressed school shootings, obesity, economic crisis and in her latest book, voluntary euthanasia. She's a contrarian, but is she also a combatant in the western world's culture wars?

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: The plight of asylum seekers in Scotland has been described as a humanitarian crisis. Sam Poling meets men and women who fled here to escape danger, only to find themselves facing a bleak and uncertain future. She travels to the illegal migrant camps in France to speak to those prepared to risk their lives to get to the UK, and asks whether the current system for those seeking refuge is fit for purpose

programme.desc: Award-winning documentary profiling Murdo MacFarlane, the Melbost Bard, the renowned Gaelic poet and songwriter from the island of Lewis, whose work was a catalyst for a whole new era of modern Gaelic music. Having composed poems and songs all his life, it was not until the advent of the tape recorder that Murdo, who could neither read nor write music, became known to a wider audience. The programme contains TV and radio archive recordings of Murdo in interviews, and also of him performing his work

programme.title: The Void

Among those taking part in the first edition are a weightlifting champion, a former US paratrooper and a professional jester

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: It's Clarkson on TV
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson takes a look at the highs and lows of the small screen, unpicking popular events and unearthing wonderful hidden gems. In this edition, he tries to save both Nigel Havers and the actor's career, examines a bucket of gravy and reveals the secret system used by lazy producers to invent atrocious TV shows

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: A look ahead to Italy v England in the showpiece match of the tournament. The Three Lions are appearing in their first-ever European Championship final, having defeated Germany, Ukraine and Denmark in the knockout rounds. The Azzurri last reached the final in 2012, but were humbled 4-0 by Spain on that occasion. Roberto Mancini's men have overcome Austria, Belgium and the Spanish to reach the final, and the four-time World Cup winners are looking to lift this trophy for the first time since 1968. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Ian Wright, Gary Neville and Roy Keane

programme.title: Bear's Mission
programme.desc: The England football manager swaps the football field for some of the UK's toughest terrain as Bear Grylls teaches him some survival skill on a mission across the wilds of Dartmoor. They begin by jumping out of either side of a helicopter, with just a single rope to slide down, before Gareth is challenged to hang on a high wire spanning a rocky gorge, 90ft in the air. But the biggest task is yet to come as Bear and the Three Lions leader abseil face first down a sheer cliff face

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the showpiece match, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. Having overcome Denmark 2-1 after extra time in the semi-finals, Gareth Southgate's men will hope to follow in the footsteps of England legends such as Bobby Moore, Geoff Hurst and Nobby Stiles by winning a major tournament at Wembley Stadium. To do so they must overcome a nation with a rich history of winning trophies, who, like England, progressed to this showpiece match with an unbeaten record, though they were made to work hard for their penalty shootout victory over Spain in the last four. The sides last met on this ground in 2018, sharing a 1-1 draw in a friendly, but both teams are much-changed since then. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville and Ian Wright, pitchside insight by Seema Jaswal, Ashley Cole and Emma Hayes, and commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: England's Grand
programme.desc: Gareth Southgate, Harry Kane and Ian Wright join a host of players, authors and experts for an A-Z journey through the history of the England football team. The documentary brings together previously unheard stories, anecdotes and unearthed archive in a celebration of the 147 years leading up to England's 1,000th match, which took place on Thursday 14th November 2019 against Montenegro

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell return to tell incredible stories of people desperate to find missing family and answer questions that have haunted entire lives. This week features two stories of children searching for their birth mothers after life-changing events - former Scotland footballer Dominic Matteo, who started to search after suffering a life-threatening brain tumour, and a woman, pained with emotion on her wedding day because her birth mother was missing

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: A look ahead to Italy v England in the showpiece match of the tournament. The Three Lions are appearing in their first-ever European Championship final, having defeated Germany, Ukraine and Denmark in the knockout rounds. The Azzurri last reached the final in 2012, but were humbled 4-0 by Spain on that occasion. Roberto Mancini's men have overcome Austria, Belgium and the Spanish to reach the final, and the four-time World Cup winners are looking to lift this trophy for the first time since 1968. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Ian Wright, Gary Neville and Roy Keane

programme.title: Bear's Mission
programme.desc: The England football manager swaps the football field for some of the UK's toughest terrain as Bear Grylls teaches him some survival skill on a mission across the wilds of Dartmoor. They begin by jumping out of either side of a helicopter, with just a single rope to slide down, before Gareth is challenged to hang on a high wire spanning a rocky gorge, 90ft in the air. But the biggest task is yet to come as Bear and the Three Lions leader abseil face first down a sheer cliff face

Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, John Lithgow, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy and Vincent Cassel

However, the star in question is in fact a beautiful woman, who he helps flee from a wicked witch and a murderous prince. Fantasy adventure, based on Neil Gaiman's novel, with Charlie Cox, Claire Danes, Michelle Pfeiffer, Robert De Niro, Mark Strong and Sienna Miller. Narrated by Ian McKellen

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie's beloved teddy bear Rupert gets recalled, and he will stop at nothing to get him back. Meanwhile, Peter falls ill and Lois takes his place at a bowling tournament, but he becomes jealous when she gets a little too close to one of his team-mates

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian writes a play that becomes a hit in Quahog, but loses his confidence when he finds that Stewie is also a budding playwright and is better at it. When the toddler sees how upset his friend is, he decides to make amends. Animation, featuring the guest voice of playwright Alan Bennett as himself

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The relationship-based reality show continues with the single people living like celebrities in the villa, all under the watchful gaze of viewers. Laura Whitmore presents

programme.title: Love Island: Aftersun
programme.desc: Laura Whitmore is joined by Munya Chawawa, HRVY and Clara Amfo to dissect all the latest news and gossip from the villa

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: When Stan lies about his texting and driving accident, he takes a seizure medication and suffers from the side effects, while Roger tries to help Steve overcome performance anxiety

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A broke Hayley gets an office job and falls for a cute colleague, prompting her to consider embarking on an affair while on a work retreat to Belize. Meanwhile, Stan chauffeurs Steve and Francine to a mother-son dance, but things turn ugly when he refuses to let them dance together

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: An outbreak of headlice at school forces Rallo to cut off his beloved Afro, and when the other kids make fun of him, Cleveland Jr steps in to look after his little brother. Meanwhile, Freight Train and Cookie leave Cleveland to house-sit for the weekend, but things go badly wrong when his friends visit

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna is annoyed when Cleveland and Cookie spend time with members of the Silver Sunset Society after they are mistaken for husband and wife. Meanwhile, Rallo steals chocolate from a street vendor, but tries to put the blame on Cleveland Junior when he is caught

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson reveals the cat with the world's longest fur, as well as attempts to spin three hula hoops on high-heeled roller skates and to push a car with a spear to the throat

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates a selection of peculiar record-breaking attempts, including the fastest blindfold 50m, the most cans crushed between the shoulder blades, the fastest tightrope walk in high heels and the world's oldest base jumper

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider asks Anne to help him find Curly. Gail worries about Sally investing all her money in Greg. Roy is furious when Mike calls Hayley a freak

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy realises he has ruined everything for Hayley. Natalie agrees to move in with Des. Gary has an audition for a band. Toyah returns from holiday with a boyfriend

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham tells Kathy he wants to see her more. Pollard and Turner try to impress Diane. Bernice throws Gavin's belongings out

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice's birthday is spoilt when she receives a card from Gavin. Emily asks Viv if she can have her job back. Diane organises a lock-in at the pub just for the girls

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick moves up a rank to Sergeant and Blaketon becomes the new postmaster, while Greengrass threatens revenge on the miscreant responsible for dosing his beloved dog Alfred with rat poison. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean become worried about their lovesick son, and invite the object of his affections to tea

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's plans for a romantic dinner for two are ruined when the Ropers have an argument. Seventies comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass starts a devious plot involving the postal service, much to Blaketon's annoyance, and several people are suspected of attacking a TV repair man. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day two of the July meeting, featuring four races, including the Falmouth Stakes. Plus, two races from York, including the 2.40 Summer Stakes. Presented by Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani, with analysis from Jason Weaver and Hayley Turner, reporting by Sally Ann Grassick and Oli Bell, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gavin tries to talk to Bernice, but she is not interested. Chris tells Zoe about a new business idea. News of Gavin's sexuality spreads around the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Diane warns Ashley to keep his distance from Bernice. Viv gives Terry a roasting for working for the Tates. Bernice turns down Alan's offer to buy the pub

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice worries about Toyah going out with yobbish Dobber. Anne admits to Spider that she set Curly up. Jim takes Liz out for dinner, but it does not go as well as he hoped

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Toyah threatens to run away again if her parents stop her from seeing Dobber. Liz is upset that Jim is still his old aggressive self. Maxine fears Audrey intends to sack her

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look at comedy in 2003, when Tim, Lee and Dawn's love triangle reached a memorable conclusion in The Office and the original run of Cold Feet bowed out with a tragic storyline. It was also the year when members of the public found themselves the unwitting comedy stooges of hidden-camera shows like Trigger Happy TV and Three Non Blondes, Bruce Forsyth made a memorable appearance as guest host of Have I Got News for You and comedy legend Bob Monkhouse performed his last stand-up gig to an audience of fellow comics in a small London pub

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Branson's political views land him in trouble and Sybil's loyalty is tested to the limit, while Ethel makes a difficult decision about her son's future. Anna wonders what is causing Bates' silence, and Carson begins recruiting a footman, with promising candidate Jimmy (Ed Speleers, Eragon) sparking immediate interest below stairs. Costume drama, starring Allen Leech, Jessica Brown Findlay, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt, Brendan Coyle and Jim Carter

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger gets a frosty response when he introduces new team member Clive to the others, while Kev and Mag present Mim with a motorised lawn-mower. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Rog gets busy organising the team's first match, while Mim tries to cheer up Bob after Ginnie gives him his marching orders. Maggie's sister inadvertently lets slip a secret - with devastating consequences. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd, determined to prove Summer can trust him, returns home and proposes to Billy. Izzy's work samples prove problematic for Carla. Fiz tells Tyrone that he needs to tell the girls about the baby today, and if he doesn't, she will

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Paul warns Billy that if he marries Todd, he will live to regret it. Carla tells Sarah that the situation with Izzy is untenable and if she refuses to come back to work, they'll have to get rid of her

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd heads home to find a hooded figure about to break into his flat. Daisy and Ryan plan their promo party in Victoria Garden for Simon's 18th birthday. Sarah tells Carla about Izzy's sudden resignation. Natasha relents and tells Nick he can see Sam

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Paul confronts Billy, demanding to know why he would let Summer hang out with Will when he nearly got her killed. Daisy makes a move on Ryan, Peter returns home from hospital, and George sings Sean's praises to a bemused Eileen

programme.title: Carry On Abroad (1972)
programme.desc: British holiday-makers jet off on a package holiday to the Spanish resort of Elsbels. Unluckily, not only is the hotel short of staff, but it also has no walls, ceilings or entertainment. Further mishaps leave the tourists fuming - until they sample a local punch laced with an aphrodisiac. Comedy, with Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A schoolgirl is found dead after a disco, and Cornish copper Charles Wycliffe applies himself to the task of discovering whether she was murdered or committed suicide. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of the sleepy hamlet of Little Worthy is disturbed when a man's body is found tied down in a miniature village - just like the hero in classic tale Gulliver's Travels. As the investigation begins, Barnaby has no shortage of suspects, including the victim's recently jilted partner - a teacher whose classroom is full of paintings of the story - his teenage lover, a caretaker and a hotel owner. But the case takes a twist when the killer strikes again amid the chaos of the annual fancy-dress boat race. Detective drama, guest starring Jesse Birdsall and Olivia Colman, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'the Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Leslie decides that he wants to be with Dionisia, and prepares to let Daphne down gently - however, it turns out that she is pregnant with his child, and her father is determined that they must get married as soon as possible. Gerry becomes fascinated by a wall in the back garden that is covered in insects

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Meena manipulates Jacob. Matty puts his foot in it. Faith is grateful

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jimmy struggles with admitting the truth. Meena is fuming, and Tracy pours out her feelings to Nate

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sparks are flying between David and Victoria. Nicola is sceptical of Bernice's advice. Ryan questions Mack's desperation

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ryan is pleased when Mack appears. Gabby is left shaken. Bernice gives unconventional marriage guidance counselling

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim makes Gabby an offer. A discovery is made. Bernice struggles

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A death in the family causes problems for Sid. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House (1972)
programme.desc: Middle-aged father Sid Abbott's dream of building an illegal whisky still in the shed is continually frustrated by the antics of his son and daughter. However, he has a bigger problem in store when new neighbours move in next door and conflict instantly erupts. Comedy, based on the sitcom, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Sally Geeson

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. As DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new forensic pathologist Kam Karimore investigate the mysterious crime, they find themselves drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching during their quest to identify the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Helen Baxendale and Navin Chowdhry

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: An Audience with Ronnie Corbett
programme.desc: The comedian entertains his peers, fans, friends and family with jokes and ramblings in a show recorded in 1997. Celebrity audience members include Donald Sinden, Martin Jarvis, Gorden Kaye, Lynda Bellingham, Norman Bowler, Kevin Lloyd and Desmond Llewelyn. Ronnie Barker also joins the host on stage to re-create some of the magic of the Two Ronnies

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd, determined to prove Summer can trust him, returns home and proposes to Billy. Izzy's work samples prove problematic for Carla. Fiz tells Tyrone that he needs to tell the girls about the baby today, and if he doesn't, she will

programme.desc: Paul warns Billy that if he marries Todd, he will live to regret it. Carla tells Sarah that the situation with Izzy is untenable and if she refuses to come back to work, they'll have to get rid of her

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A schoolgirl is found dead after a disco, and Cornish copper Charles Wycliffe applies himself to the task of discovering whether she was murdered or committed suicide. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of the sleepy hamlet of Little Worthy is disturbed when a man's body is found tied down in a miniature village - just like the hero in classic tale Gulliver's Travels. As the investigation begins, Barnaby has no shortage of suspects, including the victim's recently jilted partner - a teacher whose classroom is full of paintings of the story - his teenage lover, a caretaker and a hotel owner. But the case takes a twist when the killer strikes again amid the chaos of the annual fancy-dress boat race. Detective drama, guest starring Jesse Birdsall and Olivia Colman, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'the Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Leslie decides that he wants to be with Dionisia, and prepares to let Daphne down gently - however, it turns out that she is pregnant with his child, and her father is determined that they must get married as soon as possible. Gerry becomes fascinated by a wall in the back garden that is covered in insects

programme.desc: Middle-aged father Sid Abbott's dream of building an illegal whisky still in the shed is continually frustrated by the antics of his son and daughter. However, he has a bigger problem in store when new neighbours move in next door and conflict instantly erupts. Comedy, based on the sitcom, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Sally Geeson

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. As DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new forensic pathologist Kam Karimore investigate the mysterious crime, they find themselves drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching during their quest to identify the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Helen Baxendale and Navin Chowdhry

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: An Audience with Ronnie Corbett
programme.desc: The comedian entertains his peers, fans, friends and family with jokes and ramblings in a show recorded in 1997. Celebrity audience members include Donald Sinden, Martin Jarvis, Gorden Kaye, Lynda Bellingham, Norman Bowler, Kevin Lloyd and Desmond Llewelyn. Ronnie Barker also joins the host on stage to re-create some of the magic of the Two Ronnies

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.desc: Paul warns Billy that if he marries Todd, he will live to regret it. Carla tells Sarah that the situation with Izzy is untenable and if she refuses to come back to work, they'll have to get rid of her

programme.desc: Arthur's other half, 'er Indoors, disappears and he finds himself under suspicion of burying her on the forecourt in order to use the insurance money to prop up his dubious business ventures. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.desc: From Canada's waterlogged wilderness come stories of a 20ft lake monster that attacks humans. For freshwater detective Jeremy Wade, investigation turns into obsession as he tries to explain widespread accounts of the aggressive serpent-like beast

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In Fiji's South Pacific paradise, people are coming under attack. Plunging off the beaten track into the remote mountains of the island's interior, freshwater detective Jeremy battles tropical storms and unforgiving jungles in an all-out search for victims, clues, and culprits. Colourful stories of monstrous fish are woven into the local folklore, but how much of this is fanciful, and how much of it is real?

programme.title: Mend It for Money
programme.desc: Britain's best makers and menders go head to head, pitching for the job of bringing some dilapidated relics back to life in return for a share of the item's sale price. Simon from Nottingham is hoping to find a furniture enthusiast to breathe new life into an unusual pair of armchairs. Ben from Leeds brings in his grandfather's old machining lathe, hoping either restorers Alastair or Bruce can help get this old lathe purring like a kitten once more. The final item is a rare and innovative wardrobe, known as a gentleman's compactum, brought in by Sean from north Wales

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first hosts in Sheffield, Ray and Chloe, reckon their French cuisine will help them bag the money, but they're in for a rocky ride. On day two, Andrew and Claire want to push the boat out for their first ever dinner party. However, criticism of their unusual menu causes Andrew to bite back, and things don't improve when he does his Mr Bean impressions. On the last night, food connoisseurs Rob and Emma plan a fine dining menu, but suffer a few unfortunate mishaps in the kitchen. It seems set to be a tight finish and remains unclear who will pocket the £1,000 prize right up to the announcement

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth explore the Staffordshire and Worcestershire Canal. They uncover the history of Stourport, which only came into being after the canal was built there, which is unique for a British town. They explore the history of carpet manufacturing in Kidderminster and meet 84-year-old Audrey, who operated a daunting power loom for much of her working life. Gyles also dons a panto costume in Stourbridge to learn more about the local legend of Dick Whittington. When the duo reach Wolverhampton, they get a taste of the Desi pub, a modern institution in the Black Country that combines Punjabi food with British booze

programme.title: Amy (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary portrait of soul, jazz and R'n'B singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, whose untimely death in July 2011 followed a well-publicised history of drugs and alcohol abuse. Director Asif Kapadia traces Amy's rise and demise over the 13 years preceding her death, telling her story via her music and autobiographical song lyrics, video footage shot by friends and family, archive television clips, plus voiceover interviews with those who were personally and professionally close to her. Contributors include Pete Doherty, Mitch Winehouse, Tony Bennett, Mark Ronson and Tyler James

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Black rhino Kitani is due to give birth at Chester Zoo and help swell the ranks of this endangered species, but, having lost three previous calves, it is a worrying time and the keepers are concerned that things aren't going to plan. The zoo also has four rare new arrivals called tenrecs, mysterious mammals from Madagascar and, with so little known about them, the keepers try everything they can to help them breed, while red river hog Mali has given birth to twins, but tearaway dad Confetti is a threat to his own babies

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Natasha Cargill wants to build a home shaped like two enormous periscopes in rural Norfolk, but to obtain planning permission, she has to ensure not only that the materials are sustainable, but also agree to measure the transportation used to deliver them. If these strict criteria are not met, she won't be allowed to live there, and to add to the pressure, she has just £330,000 to spend on construction and six months to complete the project. Kevin McCloud follows her progress

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: It's springtime at the chateau, and with the wedding season just a few weeks away, completing phase two of the basement project is becoming an urgent priority for Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree. To cater for all their summer guests, a much-needed utility and laundry room is about to be plumbed in. Angel comes up with a unique way of preserving the 150-year-old wallpaper, while Dick wrestles with a rake while working on the meadow

programme.title: Scotland's Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels the beautiful coastal railways of Scotland, and meets the vibrant characters who live and work along its shoreline. In North Berwick she chats to Jane McMinn, IT consultant-turned-master mariner and lobster breeder. Other destinations include Edinburgh, where Julie discovers the intriguing connections between the railway and the famous Balmoral Hotel's Palm Court and Clock Tower

programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale

My House Is Haunted, Celebrity Karaoke Club. The also assess BBC news coverage of the England football team at Euro 2020

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Michael, a 23-year-old neuroscientist and self-confessed geek, goes on a date with publishing assistant Hannah, who is hoping her dream man exists outside of her romantic novels. However, following through with their plan would mean sacrificing room height, leading to the development of a radical solution. He also visits a family planning to transform a dilapidated swimming pool into a sunken garden room, and heads to a Second World War decontamination bunker that has found a new lease of life as a luxury dwelling. programme.title: Scotland: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: It's autumn in Scotland and the clock is ticking. As daylight diminishes and the weather cools, food resources dwindle. Animals must either migrate now or prepare to face the winter head on. Autumn sees the first sprinkling of snow on Scotland's highest peaks. Inland moorlands turn from pink to brown as heather flowers fade

programme.title: Wonderful Wales with Michael Ball
programme.desc: The singer and broadcaster explores his own heritage while discovering more about Wales's rich history. He begins in south Wales. His mother grew up in the Cynon Valley, and his grandfather and great-grandfather were both coal-miners. Michael visits the area that supplied coal to power the Titanic, and on the Gower Peninsula, he learns about cockle harvesting with three generations from Selwyn's Seafoods

programme.title: Drain the Oceans

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: The family struggles through two devastating winter storms, during which 100mph winds batter the farm and more than a month's worth of rainfall occurs in the space of just 24 hours. While Clive and Reuben brave the elements to keep the animals fed, Amanda can do nothing to stop the livestock trailer being washed away downstream in a flood. Once the first wave of extreme weather is over, they face a heavy snowfall. On the plus side, this new turn in the conditions gives Miles the chance to practise snowboarding

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: The family tackles life in lockdown, just as lambing season gets into full swing. Violet and Clemmy help to deliver some new additions to the flock, while Sydney heads out onto the moors on a mission to save a vulnerable newborn lamb. Amanda seeks to document their daily lives under lockdown with a video diary, enlisting the kids as camera crew - which results in an unusual toddlers' eye view of the farm. As the temperature rises in the spring, the family takes advantage of the good conditions to go horse riding and wild swimming

programme.title: Beatrice and Eugenie: Pampered Princesses?
programme.desc: A profile of the sisters who have been vilified, mocked, satirised and trolled for their clothes, looks, privilege and holidays, but probably most of all for simply being the children of the Duke and Duchess of York. The documentary looks at how close to reality public perception of the princesses actually is, with archive footage and interviews with royal insiders and people who know them

programme.title: The Curse of Grace Kelly's Children
programme.desc: Exploring the privileged, but blighted lives of Princess Caroline, Prince Albert and Princess Stephanie, asking why they have been plagued by controversy and tragedy. Grace Kelly was killed in a car accident, and the ripples of such a devastating loss left their mark on all three of her children, including Caroline, who was thrust into first lady duties on the death of her mother, and has struggled to find happiness

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A toddler is rushed in with suspected sepsis, a life-threatening condition that attacks the organs, and a large team of specialists administer high-strength antibiotics. Elsewhere, advanced nurse practitioner Cheryl is looking after 57-year-old Michael, who has come in with severe abdominal pain, and an 87-year-old woman is rushed in after being knocked over by a car

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates the stories of epic train journeys through stunning scenery and natural beauty, beginning with a trip from Inverness to Edinburgh. Not long after setting out, the route passes Culloden, the site of the last pitched battle on British soil. It then enters the Cairngorms National Park, home to some of Europe's finest landscapes as well as valleys steeped in Gaelic folklore. The park's station at Aviemore is home to the Strathspey Railway, where Victorian steam locomotives come to life. There is then a climb to the highest point on Britain's railway network before the train winds its way through Fife. Eventually the route crosses the Forth Bridge before arriving in Scottish capital Edinburgh, admired for its architecture and annual arts festival. Narrated by Bill Nighy
