Teenager Leon undergoes a CT scan after crashing into a central reservation. While waiting for the results, his mother Janey thinks back to his school days

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priests organise a raffle to raise funds to repair their leaky roof, planning to award a car as the prize. Unfortunately, Ted accidentally wrecks it before the grand draw takes place, and embarks on a frantic search for a replacement. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon play the Craggy Island clerics

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Sin City Motors
programme.desc: Reality TV show following the exploits of eccentric master fabricator Steve Darnell, who invites cameras into his workshop as he builds customised cars from junk

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys get their fill of petroliana at a pick in Vermont, and Frank and Dave head to Florida in search of a super-rare cycle car

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: One unit in Cardiff has the bidders all gagging. Sam is determined not to be outbid by the other women and Scotty and John try to fathom why Heavy D has come dressed as a Ninja

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: In Scunthorpe, Ione's head for heights is tested, while Barry and Darren try strongarm tactics to put the other bidders off. Sean Kelly hosts

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys land a major pick for Dolly Parton's theme park, Dollywood, but with just two weeks to find countless items, they'll need to work more than 9 to 5

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: A creepy collection of kitsch is the first in a long line of bizarre finds on Frank and Dave's picking trip. Meanwhile, Mike prepares for the shop's grand opening

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A Cuban woman crosses the bridge in the hope of being granted political asylum in America, while officers in Laredo stop three students carrying something unusual in their car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness each buy a sporty insurance write-off for £6,000 and take part in three challenges at the programme's test track before they're ordered to make their cars road legal and report to Alexandra Palace, where the producers have constructed a monstrous Wall of Death. The trio also celebrate the era of the 200mph supercar by going on a road trip in a Ferrari F40, a Lamborghini Diablo, and a Jaguar XJ220

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef continues his exploration of the Far East's most diverse food cultures, travelling down the Mekong River to Vietnam, where the local diet features an abundance of herbs, freshwater fish and flavoursome soups. As well as following in the footsteps of his hero Graham Greene in Saigon, he is reunited with a former employee in Hanoi to try out the capital city's street food, and samples the produce on offer at the night-time stalls in Bangkok, Thailand

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Cyprus on a budget rental car adventure and sample the exciting new sport of car water-skiing. Back at the test track, Chris and Paddy check out the latest family cars from Audi and Lamborghini

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus, a tribute to Stirling Moss.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts the classic improv show, with regulars Wayne Brady , Jonathan Mangum, Colin Mochrie and Ryan Stiles

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Improv comedy show with Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles, Jeff Davis, Brad Sherwood and Aisha Tyler and a special appearance by Cheryl Hines

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites comedians Lucy Porter and Romesh Ranganathan and TV judge Robert Rinder to express their anger over people, events and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include lavish proposals, school reunions and New Year's Eve

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry is joined by guests Ruby Wax, Ross Noble, Sean Lock and regular panellist Alan Davies for the quiz with a difference. The host asks questions on the subject of animal names that begin with the letter H, awarding points for the answers he finds the most interesting

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Matt Lucas, Holly Walsh and Susan Calman join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig opens her post in a post-themed edition. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Everyone gathers in Billericay as Pete and Dawn prepare to renew their wedding vows. Gavin feels uncomfortable about Gwen and Bryn knowing the details of his sex life, and Dave hears about the shocking events of the curry night. Meanwhile, Smithy goes beyond the call of friendship when he makes a staggering suggestion

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Bank Holiday Monday sees the Essex contingent arrive for a sunny day at the beach in Barry. Gavin has a heart-to-heart with his dad, which gives him much to think about, while a dark cloud looms on the horizon when Dave arrives to find Nessa and Smithy enjoying all the fun of the fair. Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden and Ruth Jones star

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Preparations for Nessa and Dave's wedding are in full swing, Stacey has life-changing news for Gavin, and Bryn and Jason put their big secret to bed once and for all. Finally, the wedding arrives and, as Nessa prepares to make her way to the church, Smithy takes the opportunity to have one last chat. Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden, Ruth Jones and Rob Brydon star

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The trio of motoring enthusiasts embark on another epic road trip, this time across western America in front-engined supercars. Jeremy Clarkson pilots the Lexus LFA, which has a spectacularly high-revving V10 powerplant, Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the muscular Dodge Viper, and James May opts for the latest incarnation of Aston Martin's Vanquish. On their journey from Las Vegas to Palm Springs via Los Angeles, the presenters tackle racing circuits and find themselves in a race against the police, before rushing to the Mexican border - and there's a terrifying penalty for the last man to arrive. Meanwhile, Fleetwood Mac drummer Mick Fleetwood rolls up his sleeves to thrash the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers become suspicious when they pull over a businessman from Mexico who is making a rare journey to the United States in a brand new car

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians James Acaster, Ed Gamble, Rhys James, Nish Kumar and Zoe Lyons. programme.desc: Leading performers on the stand-up circuit share their light-hearted views on modern life in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Tonight, the host Henning Wehn welcomes Lucy Porter and Guz Khan to the stage

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain and regulars Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Rob Beckett, Gary Delaney, Sara Pascoe and Romesh Ranganathan for the topical comedy quiz. The panellists give their take on the week's major news stories and participate in a series of stand-up spots and improvised games. First aired in 2014

programme.desc: The comedian tries his hand at being a wedding planner. programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Driving up Madagascar's eastern coast at the tail end of the rainy season is enough to test the best of friendships, but comedy actor Angus Deayton and broadcaster Mariella Frostrup are game enough to take on the challenge, and good enough friends to poke fun at each other along the way. The route has numerous river crossings and the duo face a variety of broken bridges and flimsy ferries as the only way across, while the roads themselves are mired with thick mud. Progress becomes increasingly painful and they are finally forced to spend a night in the car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a race from London's Wembley Stadium to Milan's San Siro, the home of Italian football giants AC and Inter. A price restriction leads Jeremy to grab the keys to a Shelby Mustang GT500, which costs the equivalent of £35,000 in America and packs a supercharged punch of more than 650bhp. James and Richard pin their hopes on the pan-European rail network in a bid to secure the prize of tickets to a top-flight football match, with nothing but a long journey home awaiting the losers. On the test track, Jeremy puts Toyota's GT86 coupe through its paces, and its promise of entertaining rear-wheel-drive handling should put a smile on his face. Plus, singer-songwriter Amy Macdonald gets to grips with the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Ayr as he embarks on the first leg of a journey through southern Scotland from west to east, admiring the granite island of Ailsa Craig in the Firth of Clyde before getting to grips with the sport of curling, with a little help from a Scottish world champion. The Ancient Society of Kilwinning Archers invites Michael to take part in a competition, at Barassie he rides the footplate of a freight train hauling coal on Scotland's oldest railway line as he heads on towards Stewarton

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through southern Scotland, with this leg beginning in the industrial town of Greenock, from where he sets sail in the world's last sea-going paddle steamer. He then sees the devastating effects of a fire at the Glasgow School of Art, heads to Blantyre to find out about the humble beginnings of a famous missionary and explorer and learns to make sourdough in a bakery in Strathaven before finishing his trip in Larkhall

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Motherwell as he continues his journey through southern Scotland, celebrating the Victorian iron and steel industry and admires one of its crowning achievements - the Forth Rail Bridge. He then visits the Falls of Clyde, which inspired Wordsworth and Coleridge, heads to Cumbernauld to learn about the origins of one of Scotland's best-selling soft drinks, and in Linlithgow he marvels at the ingenuity of the engineers who built the Union Canal

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey in Stirling, where he visits the scene of the Battle of Bannockburn before travelling north to Crieff in Perth and Kinross to take in a Victorian spa. In Perth, he finds out how to make a sporran and catches the Highland Main Line to Pitlochry and one of Queen Victoria's favourite haunts, then finishes with a dram in Scotland's smallest distillery

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the final leg of his journey across southern Scotland from west to east, beginning by paying homage to the birthplace of golf at St Andrews. In Dunfermline, he learns about the humble origins of a remarkable philanthropist who worked on the railroads before making his fortune in steel, and then crosses the Forth Bridge to end his trip in Edinburgh, where he treads the boards in an unconventional adaptation of a play by Oscar Wilde at the Festival Fringe

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a series of journeys through London and its environs, beginning in the Buckinghamshire market town of Amersham, where he discovers the foundations of modern-day suburbia. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels on London's first railway and admires the remarkable brick viaduct on which it was built, before taking a tour underneath its arches with a Victorian map showing the poverty of those who once lived there. programme.desc: Radio expert Mark Stuckey has a job on his hands, repairing a 1930s transistor radio brought into the Repair Shop by David Hill from Brecon. The radio was given to David's father by an army officer on the beaches at Dunkirk during the Second World War, and after being successfully shepherded through the bullets and the crashing waves to safety, it became the Hill family's pride and joy. However, the subsequent years have not been kind, with the vintage transistor battered and broken both inside and out. programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces

programme.desc: In London, Nathan and manageress Debbs do deals on an unusual triplet bike, some valuable inherited pens and a vintage silver biscuit tin. programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.desc: Paul and Clare are looking for a holiday home in central Portugal, with Paul's passion for biking a key part of their search. Jasmine Harman sets out to find a place that can double up as a bikers' retreat, on their £120,000 budget

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Nathan and Amye as they build a giant cathedral-like property modelled on Dutch barn-style houses, but with a contemporary twist. It is a hugely ambitious plan, and with Nathan project managing and setting themselves just a year to finish the build, the pressure to pull it off is huge

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Natasha Cargill wants to build a home shaped like two enormous periscopes in rural Norfolk, but to obtain planning permission, she has to ensure not only that the materials are sustainable, but also agree to measure the transportation used to deliver them. If these strict criteria are not met, she won't be allowed to live there, and to add to the pressure, she has just £330,000 to spend on construction and six months to complete the project. Kevin McCloud follows her progress

programme.title: Amy (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary portrait of soul, jazz and R'n'B singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, whose untimely death in July 2011 followed a well-publicised history of drugs and alcohol abuse. Director Asif Kapadia traces Amy's rise and demise over the 13 years preceding her death, telling her story via her music and autobiographical song lyrics, video footage shot by friends and family, archive television clips, plus voiceover interviews with those who were personally and professionally close to her. Contributors include Pete Doherty, Mitch Winehouse, Tony Bennett, Mark Ronson and Tyler James

programme.title: 100 Vaginas
programme.desc: Documentary artist Laura Dodsworth photographs 100 women and interviews 18 of the participants, aged between 19 and 77, and asks them about how their vagina has shaped their lives. They share their experiences of starting periods and pubic hair grooming, the trauma of rape and FGM, and the joy of orgasms

programme.title: Scotland's Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels the beautiful coastal railways of Scotland, and meets the vibrant characters who live and work along its shoreline. In North Berwick she chats to Jane McMinn, IT consultant-turned-master mariner and lobster breeder. Other destinations include Edinburgh, where Julie discovers the intriguing connections between the railway and the famous Balmoral Hotel's Palm Court and Clock Tower

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth explore the Staffordshire and Worcestershire Canal. They uncover the history of Stourport, which only came into being after the canal was built there, which is unique for a British town. They explore the history of carpet manufacturing in Kidderminster and meet 84-year-old Audrey, who operated a daunting power loom for much of her working life. Gyles also dons a panto costume in Stourbridge to learn more about the local legend of Dick Whittington. When the duo reach Wolverhampton, they get a taste of the Desi pub, a modern institution in the Black Country that combines Punjabi food with British booze

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: In Lanzarote, Brendan oversees a game of beach volleyball, before the group take part in a diving competition

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Gary have decided to change everything for a new life in Spain, arriving with just two suitcases, a hotel for a few nights and £3,500. But having never been to Benidorm before, could their dream turn into a nightmare? Scotsman Graham has hit upon the idea of selling organised pub crawls, while another Graham - the drivetime DJ at Fresh Radio - faces a dilemma when he is offered his old job back, which would mean spending less time with girlfriend Jackie

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Emmotts and Blakes from Surrey swap homes and budgets for the week - and although they live just 12 miles apart, their financial circumstances differ greatly. The Emmotts live in a four-bedroom house in Epsom. They enjoy various activities, while their sons are into sports and music. Their weekly budget is more than £1,300. Single mum Niki Blake, meanwhile, lives in a small flat in Addleston with her children Mia and JJ. After bills, they get by on just £125 a week

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A critically ill patient is brought into the emergency department after suffering a sudden cardiac arrest while in a local cafe with his wife. Meanwhile, doctor Bharath Reddy treats Maxine, who broke her wrist when she slipped de-icing the car, and another patient is treated after fracturing her shoulder

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In Bradford, a stolen car speeds at 100mph through the city as police officers track and pursue it, then forcibly stop the delinquent driver. In Leeds, a car grabs the attention of cops because where they expect to see a middle-aged woman driving, there is instead a teenager

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire's road crime team chases a gang stealing 4x4s from industrial estates every night, while West Yorkshire traffic cops pursue stolen vehicles across Bradford. In Leeds, a 19-year-old risks running across a busy road to meet his girlfriend and the resulting incident requires the full range of emergency services - including an air ambulance - to be deployed to try to save his life

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Barry Peach outsmarts a disqualified driver, handler Jon Peacock takes his new sniffer dog Jack to carry out a drugs warrant, Carl Warrener makes a jaw-dropping discovery when he pulls a car over in the early hours and Brian Conville gets mobbed when he deals with a reckless learner driver

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Barry Peach hunts for a man wanted for assault, officers are hot on the heels of a suspected drug dealer, a drunken driver tries every trick in the book to avoid giving a breath sample and dog-handler Jon Peacock has a late-night encounter with two unusual law-breakers

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan Brigginshaw is kept on his toes when a suspect does a runner, a routine vehicle check turns into a drug bust for Suzie Ranyard, and Barry Peach has his hands full when a group of Scottish revellers clash with bouncers in Skegness. Elsewhere, Jim Woolerton and Jason Baxter catch out a rookie rider who is breaking the law

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly

Another candidate for retraining is an energetic cavapoochon who loves to run faster than its owners can handle and does not know to respond to its own name

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits in Blackpool
programme.desc: Documentary following Britons living the high life on a low budget in one of the country's favourite resorts, from holiday-makers to those who have made the Lancashire town their home. Entertainer Simon Green - aka Betty Legs Diamond - talks about his life as a drag queen, and Scottish sisters Agnes and Margaret get more than they bargained for as part of their holiday package. Meanwhile, Jamie Diggins, who works as an estate agent for caravans, takes delivery of a static home that costs an amazing £280,000

programme.desc: Jessica attends the funeral of an old friend, whose former lover interrupts the service to insist he did not die of a heart attack, as the medical examiner's report claims, but was actually murdered. When the corpse is found to have been misidentified and a large sum of investment money goes missing from the town, the sleuth agrees that something far more complex is going on. Starring Angela Lansbury, Tom Bosley, Carrie Snodgress and Audrey Landers

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is upset when she learns that a short story of hers will appear in a magazine that has become known for its racy pictures of young women. Things take a dark turn when the publisher is found murdered and the sleuth finds herself investigating his mysterious death. Starring Angela Lansbury, Robert Stack and Bert Convy

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth investigates the murder of a tennis player's boyfriend, who was killed by a bomb planted in the sportswoman's car. Starring Angela Lansbury, Linda Hamilton and Bryan Cranston

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A reveller is murdered at the New Orleans Mardi Gras, and suspicion falls on Jessica's cousin Calhoun. Luckily, the amateur sleuth just happens to be paying a visit and sets about finding the real killer. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher tries to unravel the mysterious theft of a painting, and also finds herself investigating the death of a friend's daughter. Starring Angela Lansbury, Norman Lloyd and Cliff Gorman

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jessica sets out to search for a long-lost relative and ends up investigating a murder at a circus in Arkansas. Jackie Cooper, Martin Balsam, Courteney Cox and Greg Evigan guest star, with Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An investment analyst is found hanged from her four-poster bed, her body covered in piercings, cuts and bruises, leading the police to conclude her death occurred during a kinky sex session. The discovery of a whip containing half a million dollars in cash intrigues the officers further - but they face the task of tracking down the woman's partner to solve the mystery. Crime drama, with Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman who claims she has been raped turns to the Unit for help, but the team cannot get enough evidence against the prime suspect and is forced to drop the case. However, another victim comes forward six months later with a similar story, making them believe they have made a terrible mistake. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A corrections officer is arrested, accused of sexually assaulting his female charges both inside the prison system and beyond. As the case against the man begins to take shape, the union that supports him takes aim at Barba and the other members of the unit. Crime drama, starring Raul Esparza

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: As the investigation of Gary Munson goes on, his union takes action and threatens the lives in the SVU

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Part one of two. Black Claw tightens its grip on Portland, making a strategic move against Hank and sending Nick on a war path. Hadrian's Wall seeks a mysterious man named Bonaparte, while Adalind and Renard learn to deal with their new and very unpredictable reality with Diana

programme.title: The Enemy Within

programme.title: Columbo: Prescription Murder (1968)
programme.desc: A psychiatrist having an affair with a patient murders his wife with his lover's help. However, while he establishes the perfect alibi he soon finds himself pursued by Columbo, whose meandering investigative approach conceals an uncanny gift for detection. First episode of the detective series, starring Peter Falk and Gene Barry

programme.title: Columbo: How to Dial a Murder (1978)
programme.desc: After discovering that his best friend and his wife were having an affair, a renowned psychologist uses his unique mind-control powers to commit a bizarre murder - with the help of two pet dogs conditioned to attack when they hear a certain command. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Nicol Williamson, Frank Aletter and Kim Cattrall

programme.title: Columbo: Murder, Smoke and Shadows (1989)
programme.desc: An ambitious Hollywood director is visited by an old friend, who threatens to expose evidence of his involvement in the death of an actor. The film-maker resorts to murder to keep his dark secret safe, only to end up on the receiving end of Columbo's distinctive brand of interrogation. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Fisher Stevens and Molly Hagan

programme.title: Columbo: Candidate for Crime (1973)
programme.desc: An ambitious senatorial candidate murders his campaign manager for threatening to expose his secret affair. To avoid suspicion, the politician makes the killing look as if he was the intended target of an assassination attempt that went wrong - but ends up having to pit his wits against the slovenly sleuth. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Jackie Cooper, Ken Swofford and Tisha Sterling

programme.title: Columbo: Death Hits the Jackpot (1991)
programme.desc: A photographer going through a divorce asks his jeweller uncle to cash in his winning lottery ticket to prevent his wife from getting a share. Seizing the opportunity to keep the money, the greedy older man kills his nephew - but has reckoned without Columbo's intervention. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Rip Torn, Gary Kroeger and Jamie Rose

programme.title: Columbo: Strange Bedfellows (1995)
programme.desc: A horse trainer outraged by his brother's gambling debts murders both him and the bookie he owes money to, then claims one killed the other. Columbo is not convinced by his story and goes in search of the truth, and finds help from an unlikely source when a Mob enforcer is sent to investigate the bookie's death. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Rod Steiger and George Wendt

programme.title: Columbo Goes to the Guillotine (1989)
programme.desc: The dishevelled detective investigates the suspicious death of a stage magician. All the evidence points toward suicide - but the sleuth is soon on the trail of an ex-convict who once conspired with the illusionist to steal military secrets. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Anthony Andrews and Karen Austin

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Benson get mixed up in the murky world of politics after a coalition leader's gay son is found murdered on the roof of his apartment. The finger of suspicion points to a notorious rapist, who has just been released from prison on parole, but he insists he didn't commit the crime - so further DNA tests are used to shed light on the death. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a promiscuous millionaire, and soon discover he had a tangled private life, involving a string of lovers and an angry wife. A team of scheming, sultry Russian gold-diggers could hold essential clues to the killing. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Benson are assigned the case of a taxi driver found stabbed to death, and it soon emerges that the victim had a selection of false identities, leading the cops on a wild goose chase before they uncover any concrete leads. First episode of the spin-off drama, starring Mariska Hargitay, Christopher Meloni and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The officers are called in to investigate when a successful newspaper columnist falls six storeys to her death, dressed only in her underwear. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Frontline Police
programme.desc: Rav Wilding accompanies Essex police on a drugs raid, but the operation turns into a chase when the suspect escapes through the back door. Paramedics rush to Epping Forest to help an unconscious man, an emergency unit struggles to get a woman to the police station, and a routine traffic stop catches a motorist who is three times over the drink-drive limit. Elsewhere, officers search for a man who has been accused of threatening his former girlfriend - and find him in a very unusual place

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: In November 1983, Dennis Nilsen was sentenced at the Old Bailey to life imprisonment for killing six men and the attempted murders of two others. This docudrama examines the events that led him to become a serial killer, using witness testimony, detailed crime reports, forensic evidence and expert commentary by criminologists, detectives, pathologists and journalists

programme.title: Border Security: Canada's Front Line

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings

programme.desc: The John Deere Classic. Coverage of the second day at TPC Deere Run in Silvis, Illinois, as the competition takes place for the first time since 2019 because of the coronavirus pandemic. South Africa's Dylan Frittelli won here two years ago with a score of 21-under-par, ending a run of 12 consecutive wins by American golfers. Steve Stricker won this tournament three times in a row between 2009 and 2011.
